paper_title: Analysis of Protein Sequence Identity, Binding Sites, and 3D Structures Identifies Eight Pollen Species and Ten Fruit Species with High Risk of Cross-Reactive Allergies

methods.method_name: Signal Peptide and Transmembrane Topology Prediction
methods.description: This method involves predicting the presence and location of signal peptide cleavage sites in amino acid sequences using SignalP 6.0, and simultaneously predicting transmembrane topology using TMHMM. This is crucial for distinguishing between signal peptides and membrane-spanning segments in proteins.

steps.description: Signal peptide analysis was performed using SignalP 6.0.
steps.searchable_summary: use SignalP 6.0 to predict signal peptide cleavage sites

steps.description: SignalP 6.0 server predicts the presence and location of signal peptide cleavage sites in amino acid sequences from different organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: predict presence and location of signal peptide cleavage sites
outputs: presence and location of signal peptide cleavage sites

steps.description: The method incorporates a prediction of cleavage sites and a signal peptide/non-signal peptide prediction based on a combination of several artificial neural networks and hidden Markov models.
steps.searchable_summary: use neural networks and HMMs for signal peptide prediction

steps.description: SignalP 6.0 predicts the regions of signal peptides, including n-, h-, and c-regions.
steps.searchable_summary: predict n-, h-, and c-regions of signal peptides
outputs: n-, h-, and c-regions of signal peptides

steps.description: Signal peptide prediction and transmembrane topology prediction were performed simultaneously by software TMHMM.
steps.searchable_summary: use TMHMM for transmembrane topology prediction

methods.description: This method estimates and visualizes the likelihood of IgE cross-reactivity between allergens using the A-RISC index, which is based on protein sequence similarity and identity. It provides a numerical value indicating the relative homology between allergens.

steps.description: We applied the A-RISC index in our study following the method proposed by Chruszcz et al. in 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: apply A-RISC index for cross-reactivity analysis

steps.description: The A-RISC index for a specific protein pair is defined as the average of protein sequence similarity (S) and identity (I).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate A-RISC index as average of similarity and identity

steps.description: The index provides information on the relative homology between allergens from a particular protein family and selected members of that family.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze relative homology using A-RISC index
